examples.title: Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic instruction cycle in a CPU
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ExecuteInstruction
BEGIN
  // FETCH PHASE
  MAR <- PC           // Load address of next instruction
  READ               // Signal memory to read
  IR <- MDR          // Load instruction into Instruction Register
  PC <- PC + 1       // Increment Program Counter
  
  // DECODE PHASE
  opcode <- IR[op]   // Extract operation code
  operand <- IR[addr] // Extract address/operand
  
  // EXECUTE PHASE
  SWITCH opcode DO
    CASE LOAD:  MAR <- operand
                READ
                ACC <- MDR
    CASE STORE: MAR <- operand
                MDR <- ACC
                WRITE
    CASE ADD:   MAR <- operand
                READ
                ACC <- ACC + MDR
    CASE JUMP:  PC <- operand
  END SWITCH
  
  // STORE PHASE (if needed)
  // Result already stored in ACC or memory
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a CPU fetches an instruction from memory, decodes it to understand what operation to perform, executes the operation, and stores the result back.

examples.title: Register Simulation in Python
examples.description: Simulates basic CPU registers and their functions
examples.code:
class CPURegisters:
    def __init__(self):
        self.PC = 0        # Program Counter
        self.IR = 0        # Instruction Register
        self.MAR = 0       # Memory Address Register
        self.MDR = 0       # Memory Data Register
        self.ACC = 0       # Accumulator
        self.status = 0   # Status/Flag Register
    
    def fetch(self, memory, address):
        """Fetch instruction from memory at given address"""
        self.MAR = address
        self.IR = memory[self.MAR]
        self.PC += 1
        return self.IR
    
    def execute_load(self, memory, address):
        """LOAD: Load data from memory to accumulator"""
        self.MAR = address
        self.MDR = memory[self.MAR]
        self.ACC = self.MDR
    
    def execute_store(self, memory, address):
        """STORE: Save accumulator data to memory"""
        self.MAR = address
        self.MDR = self.ACC
        memory[self.MAR] = self.MDR
    
    def execute_add(self, memory, address):
        """ADD: Add memory value to accumulator"""
        self.MAR = address
        self.MDR = memory[self.MAR]
        self.ACC = self.ACC + self.MDR

# Example usage
cpu = CPURegisters()
memory = [0] * 256
memory[0] = 200  # LOAD instruction
memory[1] = 10   # Address 10
memory[10] = 25  # Data value

cpu.fetch(memory, 0)  # Fetch instruction at address 0
cpu.execute_load(memory, 1)  # Load from address 1
print(f"Accumulator: {cpu.ACC}")  # Output: 25
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates a simple CPU with registers and demonstrates how instructions are fetched and executed using the fetch-decode-execute cycle.

examples.title: Instruction Decode and Execute in C
examples.description: Shows how machine instructions are decoded and executed
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates how a CPU decodes machine instructions by extracting the opcode and address fields, then executes the corresponding operation.
